title: tephrosia domingensis
intro: Tephrosia domingensis is a species of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae, commonly known as the West Indian horsefly tree or horsefly tree, due to its habit of attracting horseflies. The plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Tephrosia domingensis include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a diuretic and to treat digestive issues. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments.
